PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NUISANCE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION CANNOT BE GRANTED TO A PORTION OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. (1) PLAINTIFFS' NUISANCE CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT DISTINGUISH BETWEEN ACCESS TO THE ELECTRICAL BREAKERS AND ACCESS TO THE GARBAGE AREA. (2) EVEN IF ACCESS TO THE ELECTRICAL BREAKERS CONSTITUTED A SEPARATE CAUSE OF ACTION, DAMAGES WERE NOT SHIFTED. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFFS' SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CC&RS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN. ADDITIONALLY, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER CC&RS ("THE DECLARATION") ENTITLE PLAINTIFFS AND THEIR TENANTS 24/7 ACCESS TO THE UTILITIES AREA. (SEE DECL. PIERCE 9-13). PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFFS' THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS DENIED, FOR THE SAME REASONS AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO DEFENDANT'S SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S THIRD AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS THE OPERATIVE CROSS-COMPLAINT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Denny (415-531-8189) for Cross Complainant/ opposing party Hilary C. Pierce.
No other appearances.

No argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Judgment or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED.Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Judgment as to Plaintiffs' Complaint is DENIED, see rulings on Motion for Summary Adjudication.
Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiffs' First Cause of Action for nuisance is DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to shift the burden. Summary adjudication cannot be granted to a portion of a cause of action. (1) Plaintiffs' nuisance cause of action does not distinguish between access to the electrical breakers and access to the garbage area. (2) Even if access to the electrical breakers constituted a separate cause of action, damages were not shifted.
Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiffs' Second Cause of Action for breach of CC&Rs is DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to shift burden. Additionally, there are triable issues of fact as to whether CC&Rs ("the Declaration") entitle Plaintiffs and their tenants 24/7 access to the Utilities Area. (See Decl. Pierce 9-13). Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiffs' Third Cause of Action for declaratory relief is DENIED, for the same reasons as to the First and Second Causes of Action. Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Judgment or Summary Adjudication as to Defendant's Second Amended Cross-Complaint is DENIED. Defendant's Third Amended Cross-Complaint is the operative cross-complaint. Plaintiffs noticed their Motion for Summary Judgment or Summary Adjudication as to Defendant's Second Amended Cross-Complaint. Order signed in Open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
